<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

The create-vite change and COPYFILE_FICLONE optimization are sound. The one real regression is in Vite's `prepareOutDir`: replacing `copyDir` with `fs.cpSync` silently drops a guard that prevented copying the output directory into itself when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir`, changing observable behavior for those users. An equivalent pre-check should be restored.

**3** finding(s): ЁЯФ┤ 0  ┬╖  ЁЯЯб 1  ┬╖  ЁЯФ╡ 2

- ЁЯЯб `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ Lost self-copy guard when replacing copyDir with fs.cpSync тАФ The old `copyDir` in `utils.ts` contained a guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that prevented copying the destination into itself when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir`. `fs.cpSync` has no equivalent guard and will recurse into the destination (e.g., `public/dist` тЖТ `public/dist/dist`), producing duplicated content or errors. An equivalent pre-check (e.g., skip the copy if `outDir` is nested under `publicDir`) should be added to preserve the old contract.

  ```suggestion
  Add a pre-check before `fs.cpSync` that skips the copy when `outDir` starts with `publicDir` + path.sep, or filter the offending entry from the source directory listing.
  ```
- ЁЯФ╡ `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ fs.cpSync emits ExperimentalWarning on Node 18тАУ22.2 тАФ The eslint-disable comment acknowledges that `fs.cpSync` with `recursive: true` was only stabilized in Node 22.3. On Node 18.x (a commonly supported minimum), it emits an `ExperimentalWarning` to stderr on every build invocation, which is noisy for end users. Worth confirming against the declared `engines.node` minimum before merging.
- ЁЯФ╡ `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ fs.cpSync emits ExperimentalWarning on older Node versions тАФ Same as the Vite note тАФ `fs.cpSync` with `recursive: true` fires an `ExperimentalWarning` on Node 18тАУ22.2. For create-vite this only fires once per scaffolding, so it is tolerable. Confirm against declared `engines.node` minimum.

---
_Reviewed by: Correctness, Security ┬╖ glm-5.2 ┬╖ findings reflect the enabled lenses only_
